method_atom: A framework for large populations of self-replicating discrete-state units evolving under mutation plus nonlinear pairwise or population-level mixing/exchange of components, generalizing linear replicator-mutation dynamics with a permutation-invariant replication-rate function. The steady state is obtained exactly through an analytic field-theoretic mapping, yielding a variational maximum principle for the equilibrium mean growth rate and phase diagrams with distinct selected phases (including a spectrally rigid one) and transitions. Whether mixing aids or hinders selection depends on the sign of the interaction terms in the rate function: cooperative (positive) interactions impose a mild load while competitive (negative) interactions enhance drive toward the optimal state.
object_atom: Genotypes of individuals in a biological population evolving by mutation and natural selection on a fitness landscape, augmented by horizontal gene transfer from the population and by sexual recombination between pairs of parents, with epistasis in the fitness landscape.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the nonlinear mutation-plus-mixing replicator dynamics solved by field-theoretic mapping and a maximum principle; the domain-specific part is the biological genotype population undergoing horizontal gene transfer and recombination on an epistatic fitness landscape.
